text: The competence and skills required for police officers in accordance with the paradigms change in the Juvenile Justice System Law Number 11 of 2012 regarding the principle of restorative justice and diversion, need to be understood properly. This research aimed to explore the steps taken by the relevant government institutions in preparing the implementation of this Law, and the obstacles faced. Data were obtained from the court investigators and prosecutors, as well as the child court judges. All of these institutions, apart from some outreach, have not yet understood or realized the concept of the implementation of this Law as they have only received information about Law Number 11 of 2012, whereas the judges have received information about the draft Law. The number of police officers that received the outreach program is not yet optimum and needs to be improved. The perception of the police regarding corruption allegations is seen as being a major obstacle to the implementation of the diversion principle based on the principle of restorative justice.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
